revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 64x64 px crisp 16-bit SNES-style pixel-art terrain tileset spritesheet. Layout is a 4 rows x 4 columns grid, 16 frames total, each tile exactly 16x16 px. Do not draw visible grid lines, borders, labels, tile IDs, text, UI, signatures, or watermark. Each frame must stay inside its own 16x16 cell, same scale, fully filled edge-to-edge as a seamless interchangeable top-down grass tile.

Asset profile: snes16_platformer_v1. Preset: world_grass_variation_16. Asset kind: terrain-tile. Asset type: tile.

Subject: bright mythical-Japan satoyama world-map grassland variation tiles. Use deep rich saturated early-summer greens like lush rice-field banks and dewy meadow grass, with soft warm sunlight, top-left light, uniform brightness above 55 percent, no hard shadows. Add subtle low-frequency regional palette drift across tiles, fine readable blade clusters, tiny clover and moss accents, occasional sparse tiny flowers, worn grass patches, grass-overlap details, chipped organic edge breakup, center/edge/corner/inner-corner-like variation while still reading as all-grass interchangeable seamless tiles. Include a few vermilion and white accent pixels only as tiny meadow flowers. Palette should be cheerful, saturated, clear, and cohesive; not dry, not yellow-brown, not western prairie, not wasteland.

Tilemap quality: avoid a repeated square stamp, avoid high-frequency checkerboard texture, avoid hard rectangular bands. The final sheet should read as one cohesive grassland tileset with natural variation and no visible checkerboard. Use crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur, consistent palette, one-pixel dark outline only where useful for small grass/flower clusters. If any empty space exists, fill it with solid #FF00FF magenta, but tiles themselves should be completely filled edge-to-edge with grass.

Avoid all characters, logos, recognizable franchise likenesses, samurai armor, kabuto helmets, ninja outfits, two-sword silhouettes, guns, modern objects, horror mood, dark gloomy colors, birds or companion creatures, letter emblems, modern text, watermark, or UI.
